claim: Norwegian GPFG sovereign wealth fund (Oljefondet, founded 1990) demonstrates that collective ownership of resource rents with market-based management can avoid resource-curse outcomes.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Norwegian GPFG sovereign wealth fund (Oljefondet, founded 1990) demonstrates that collective ownership of resource rents with market-based management can avoid resource-curse outcomes.

claim: Nationalisation of producing oil, gas, and mining enterprises without preservation of operational autonomy reduces extractor output within 3–5 years of nationalisation and underperforms the counterfactual trajectory for at least a decade. Documented cases: Venezuela PDVSA 2003-2007 (purge of technical staff, subsequent 50%+ output collapse through 2020), Argentina YPF 2012 (expropriation from Repsol, subsequent capex retreat), Bolivia YPFB 2006 (gas output stagnation vs regional trend), Mexico Pemex (post-1938 long decline in extraction efficiency), UK 1970s coal + steel nationalisations (productivity decline), Iran post-1979. The contrast case the framework preserves is market-respecting state ownership (Norway Statoil/Equinor, Saudi Aramco pre-2019, Gulf NOCs with technocratic autonomy) — where state ownership with commercial operational rules does NOT reduce output. The discriminating variable is preservation of technocratic autonomy + commercial incentive structure, not ownership per se.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Resource-extractor nationalisation with autonomy loss reduces output.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Chang argues strategic state ownership at development phase is net-positive if technocratically operated.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Resource-extractor nationalisation can produce desired outcomes if aligned with ecological mandate.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Eco-socialist position accepts output decline when output reduction IS the policy goal.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Resource-extractor nationalisation with autonomy preservation produces output-neutral outcomes.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Framework predicts decline if autonomy-lost; market socialism predicts the autonomy-preservation boundary holds.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Resource-extractor nationalisation reduces output (even with autonomy-claim).
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: ML position predicts state ownership with planning discipline produces output growth; framework predicts decline.
